steps: Thaw oligo deep well plate at 500 rcf, 4 °C, 30 minutes , in swing-bucket centrifuge.
Thaw T4 DNA Ligase Reaction Buffer - 6.0 mL On ice with T4 Polynucleotide Kinase - 2,500 units.
For 1 reaction of PA mix in 1.5 mL tube: 1 µL T4 DNA Ligase Reaction Buffer - 6.0 mL 6.5 µL UltraPure™ DNase/RNase-Free Distilled Water 0.5 µL T4 Polynucleotide Kinase - 2,500 units

For dispensing PA mix to a 96 wells of a 96-well or 384-well plate using the Mantis (10% overage) mix: Hard Shell 384-well Plate 105.6 µL T4 DNA Ligase Reaction Buffer - 6.0 mL 686.4 µL UltraPure™ DNase/RNase-Free Distilled Water 52.8 µL T4 Polynucleotide Kinase - 2,500 units. Using the attached "PA mix" template for the Mantis will dispense appropriate volumes of water and PA mastermix in the correct wells as described below. Water and PA mix can both be loaded onto the for sequential dispensing.

Add 5 mL water to a 15 mL conical tube and attach Mantis HV tubing (green) - one end of tubing should be at the base of the conical tube, and the other end of the tubing should attach to a HV chip in the mantis.

Dispense 9 µL water into well position 2, and 19 µL water into position 3. Alternatively, a 1000 µL pipette tip can be filled with water and attached directly to the HV chip . The dispense will need to be paused to refill the water after 128 wells (64 9 µL wells and 64 19 µL wells) and after 256 wells have been filled. Note: If not using HV tubing , use the "PA mix - tip only" template for the Mantis to automatically pause for water refills.
Add all 844.8 µL PA mix to a 1000 µL pipette tip and carefully attach the tip to a labeled LV Mantis chip.
Dispense 9 µL PA mix into well position 1.
Seal with a foil seal.
Spin down the plate at 500 rcf, 4 °C, 1 hour . In a PCR hood:
Attach 20 µL tip box to Liquidator, making sure to tamp down to fully attach tips.
Remove the seal from both the deep well oligo plate and the 384-well plate.

Place the deep well plate on the left in front of the attached tips, and the 384-well plate on its adapter on the right. Note: If forward and reverse oligos are stored in separate plates, two sets of Liquidator tips must be used to prevent cross contamination. Only 1 µL of each separate oligo is needed.
Using the Liquidator, take 2 µL paired oligos and transfer them into the PA mix at well position 1.
Change the Liquidator volume to 5 µL and mix by pipetting up and down.
Seal plate with foil seal.
If no bubbles have been introduced by mixing, it is not necessary to spin the plate down again. If they have, spin down the plate at 500 rcf, 4 °C, 1 hour .
Place the sealed plate into a thermal cycler and select the "PA Mix" assay. 37 °C 00:30:00 95 °C 00:05:00 Ramp down to 25 °C at 5 °C per 00:01:00 . Hold at 10 °C .
Transfer plate back to PCR hood.
Take 1 µL PA mix from well position 1 and transfer to well position 2. Mix at 5 µL .
Take 1 µL from well position 2 and transfer to well position 3. Mix at 10 µL . This performs a 1:200 dilution.

For one reaction of Digestion-Ligation GoldenGate ( DL ) Mix: 2 µL T4 DNA Ligase Reaction Buffer - 6.0 mL 1 µL BbsI-HF - 1,500 units 1 µL T4 DNA Ligase - 100,000 units 1 µL PX45-sfGFP at 100 ng/µL (or equivalent) 14 µL water or up to 19 µL total volume if using different concentration plasmid.

For dispensing DL mix to a 96 wells of a 96-well or 384-well plate using the Mantis (10% overage): 211.2 µL T4 DNA Ligase Reaction Buffer - 6.0 mL . 105.6 µL BbsI-HF - 1,500 units 105.6 µL T4 DNA Ligase - 100,000 units 105.6 µL PX45-sfGFP at 100 ng/µL (or equivalent) 1478.4 µL water or up to 2006.4 µL total volume if using different concentration plasmid.
Using a mantis HV chip and a 1000 µL pipette tip, dispense 19 µL DL mix into well position 4 of the 384-well plate, or into a new 96-well plate with the "DL mix" and "DL mix 96-well" templates.
The Mantis will fill half the plate, then pause.
Keep the remaining DL mix On ice until you are able to repeat the dispense across the second half of the plate.
Spin down the plate at 500 rcf, 4 °C, 1 hour . In a PCR hood:
Using the Liquidator, transfer 1 µL diluted oligo duplex from well position 3 into DL mix in well position 4.

Mix gently by pipetting up and down at 10 µL . If no bubbles have been introduced by mixing, it is not necessary to spin the plate down again. If they have, spin down the plate at 500 rcf, 4 °C, 1 hour .
Incubate in QuantStudio 6 Pro if using 384-well, or any standard thermal cycler if using a 96-well
10 cycles of: 00:10:00 37 °C digestion 00:10:00 16 °C ligation
Followed by: 00:05:00 50 °C final digestion 00:05:00 80 °C enzyme inactivation (enzyme inactivation) hold at 12 °C hold
Place 2 LB + Amp Omni agar plates to pre-warm in an incubator set at 37 °C .
Prepare 2 96-well plates with 195 µL LB or SOC in each well. These can be sealed and pre-warmed as well.
Thaw 96-wellStellar Competent Cells On ice . Note: Ligations can be stored at 4 °C . If fewer transformations are desired, the Stellar cells can be split along perforations for 24 wells (3 by 8).
Using the Liquidator, transfer 2 µL ligated DL mix into the cells.
Briefly mix by pipetting up and down at 5 µL .
Heat shock cells at 42 °C for 00:00:30 and return to On ice for 00:03:00 .
Using the 5 µL - 200 µL Liquidator, add 80 µL of LB or SOC from the first pre-warmed plate.
Mix gently by pipetting up and down at 50 µL .
Using the 5 µL - 200 µL Liquidator, transfer 5 µL of diluted media into the second pre-warmed plate.
Mix gently by pipetting up and down at 100 µL .
Using the 2 µL - 20 µL Liquidator, transfer 1 µL diluted transformations to first agar plate, and 3 µL diluted transformations to the second agar plate.
In a PCR hood or underneath a Bunsen burner, allow media droplets to dry before flipped plates and transferring to an incubator at 37 °C Overnight .
Successful digestion - ligations of PX45-sfGFP will have exchanged GFP for the sgRNA oligo duplex and will no longer fluoresce under blue light.
Pick GFP negative colonies and transfer to 150 mL LB + Amp in shaker flask.
Grow Overnight 250 rpm, 37 °C,
Perform plasmid DNA extraction according to ZymoPURE II Plasmid Maxiprep Kit.
Sanger sequence using standard U6 promoter sequencing primer.
creator: Nahum Smith
